title: bignonia heterophylla
intro: Bignonia heterophylla, also known as the False Jesuit's Bark or Trumpet Vine, is a flowering vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Bignonia heterophylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe sore throats. In herbal preparations, it is often used as a tea or tincture, and can also be found in combination with other herbs to create syrups and cough medicines.
